active_blocker: A733 SDMMC0 now proves card init, pre-mmcblk CMD17, small PIO CMD18 reads, partition discovery, CMD49 cache-enable via a scoped PIO write, and read-only EXT4 root mount to init=/bin/sh when block reads are capped to 8 blocks. H008 proves CMD49 can complete through the narrow PIO write path while all other writes remain guarded. The unresolved hardware issue remains IDMA-backed transfer handling; the next low-risk test is a single-descriptor IDMA CMD18 read with the proven PIO safety rails still present.

operator_model.restore_after_test: Power-cycle Cubie3 and verify vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733 before ending the session.

session_exit_gates.hypothesis_queue_update_committed: Update and commit this file. Sync it to /srv/projects/homelab/task-packets/kernel/a733-hypothesis-queue.json on Strix.
session_exit_gates.remote_push_checked: Push repos with configured remotes and record any repo that has no remote.

guardrails: Do not add vendor U-Boot compatibility strings, aliases, paths, or policy nodes to upstream DTS.
Do not expand into Ethernet, VPU, display, USB-C, PCIe, or wireless.
Do not submit local A733 CCU or pinctrl scaffolding while Junhui Liu's CCU/PRCM RFC and Andre Przywara's pinctrl RFC are in flight.
Keep clk_ignore_unused and PIO bypasses as lab diagnostics only.

latest_completed_test.title: Controlled CMD49 PIO cache-enable proof
latest_completed_test.result: Passed. CMD49 cache-enable ran through the scoped A733 PIO write path with opcode=49, one 512-byte block, and pio=1. The card then enumerated as mmcblk0, partitions p1/p2/p3 appeared, EXT4 mounted read-only on mmcblk0p3, and init=/bin/sh started. No diag block A733 write or opcode=25 write appeared in the captured proof window. This proves CMD49 write protocol mechanics without enabling general writes.

hypotheses.title: Fixed vendor-vs-mainline data-phase register comparison before another boot variant
hypotheses.question: Which visible SDMMC0 register differs at the same data-phase moment where vendor advances CHDA/CBDA and mainline remains in descriptor-read state?
hypotheses.test_cost: low; source/log analysis first, no kernel build required
procedure: Read the vendor SDMMC0 wide register sample and the latest mainline UART log.
Fill task-packets/kernel/a733-sdmmc-register-dump-template.json for the phases vendor-working-read, mainline-dma-entry, mainline-idma-post-cmdr, and mainline-stall-poll.

Compare GCTRL, CLKCR, WIDTH, CMDR, CARG, IMASK, RINTR, STAS, NTSR, FTRGL, THLD, DMAC, DLBA, IDST, IDIE, CHDA, CBDA, BLKSZ, BCNTR, CBCR, BBCR, FUNS, DBGC, CSDC, DRV_DL, SAMP_DL, DS_DL, SKEW_CTRL, and SMCV.
If a single non-cosmetic delta remains, create the next minimal patch from that delta only. If no visible delta remains, move to H002.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: A register delta is identified that plausibly controls data/DMA progress and is absent from prior falsified tests.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: No visible register delta remains; next work must inspect hidden wrapper/source behavior instead of cycling another visible-register boot variant.

result.summary: Most visible setup registers match: CMDR, FTRGL, THLD, DMAC, IDIE, FUNS, DRV_DL, NTSR, SMCV. Vendor advances CHDA/CBDA and data counters, while mainline remains at CHDA=DLBA and CBDA=0. The only setup-looking visible delta is SAMP_DL=0x00002000 on vendor versus 0 on mainline, but the raw SAMP_DL write was already tested and did not stick. Do not create another visible-register boot variant without source evidence.

hypotheses.title: Find hidden v5p3x wrapper, timing-phase, or MBUS/storage behavior in vendor source
hypotheses.question: Does the vendor A733 v5p3x path program a non-SDMMC register, wrapper bit, or timing phase path required before IDMAC can fetch descriptors?
hypotheses.test_cost: low to medium; source search before build
partial_results: Orange Pi A733 v5p3x source has a direct REG_SAMP_DL programming block, but it is commented out.
The active vendor phase path writes REG_SD_NTSR and saves/restores REG_SAMP_DL; the v5p3x init path does not assign the sunxi_mmc_set_samp_dl hook.
The preserved-SAMP_DL diagnostic proves SAMP_DL=0x00002000 survives into the failing mainline CMD18, but IDMAC still sticks in descriptor-read state.
Explicit SDMMC0 consumers for mmc_store, mmc_mbus, and mmc_msi_lite were already tested and did not fix IDMA.
Vendor A733 DTS uses CLK_MSI_LITE1 for SDMMC0; the local diagnostic changed only the SDMMC0 mmc_msi_lite consumer to CLK_BUS_MSI_LITE1 and still hit the same CMD18 IDMA descriptor-read stall.
Vendor SDMMC0 DTS sets req-page-count=<2>; changing local descriptor allocation to two pages produced descriptor sync size=8192 and still hit the same CMD18 IDMA descriptor-read stall.

A source scan found no active A733 runtime wrapper write outside the SDMMC register window. The ioremap writes in the available MMC vendor code are FPGA/SUN300/CQE or commented diagnostic paths, not the Cubie A7S SDMMC0 runtime path.

Vendor ctl-spec-caps=<0x428> maps to retry, manual read-data-timeout, and CMD11 timeout handling. The vendor baseline still shows TMOUT=0xffffffff at idle, and this path is not a plausible descriptor-fetch enable.
procedure: Continue the vendor/source search outside the already-falsified direct SAMP_DL, sample-calibration, and explicit mmc_store/mmc_mbus/mmc_msi_lite paths.
Search for SDMMC0, v5p3x, IDMAC, MBUS, MSI-lite, storage fabric, wrapper register writes outside the SDMMC register window, cache/MBUS attributes, and any A733-specific IDMAC enable sequence.
Record file, function, register address, value, and call path.
Only if a concrete write is found, build a minimal diagnostic that adds that one write with printk evidence.
Boot Cubie3 once, capture UART, restore vendor kernel, and update this queue.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: A specific hidden register/write is found and tested.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: No source-backed hidden write exists in available vendor code.
hypotheses.result: No active A733/Cubie3 SDMMC0 hidden wrapper write was found in the available vendor runtime path. Source-backed subtests for SAMP_DL preservation, SDMMC0 MSI Lite1 clock mapping, and exact SDMMC0 req-page-count descriptor allocation were all falsified. Move to the lower-level CMD17 proof.

hypotheses.title: Lower-level CMD17 proof outside mmcblk queue setup
hypotheses.question: Can a single-block read complete cleanly when issued as a targeted diagnostic instead of forcing block-layer queue geometry?
hypotheses.test_cost: medium; small kernel patch and one boot
procedure: Do not change global mmcblk queue limits.
Add a one-shot diagnostic after card init that issues or emulates one controlled 512-byte read and logs RINTR, STAS, FIFO, and data bytes.
Boot once and capture UART.
Restore Cubie3 and update the queue.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: Controlled CMD17 completes and returns data, separating single-block protocol from CMD18/multi-block behavior.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: CMD17 also stops at command-done/RX-request with no data-over.

result.summary: Passed. Pre-mmcblk CMD17 completed with ret=0 and 512 bytes copied from FIFO. The data ended with 0xaa55, then mmcblk0 and partitions p1/p2/p3 appeared. Small PIO CMD18 reads of 8 blocks also completed. The remaining failure is the later 256-block IDMA CMD18.

hypotheses.title: PIO-sized block-layer rootfs proof
hypotheses.question: Can Cubie3 mount the SD rootfs if block-layer reads are constrained to the small PIO-sized request shape that H003 proved works?
hypotheses.test_cost: medium; small kernel patch and one boot
procedure: Keep this as a lab diagnostic only; do not present it as an upstream fix.
Patch only the A733 diagnostic path so SDMMC0 advertises small block-layer limits, preferably max_blk_count=8, max_segs=1, max_seg_size=4096, and max_req_size=4096.
Keep the existing PIO path for reads with blocks<=8 and blksz=512.
Build Image/DTB from Strix, install into Cubie3 /boot/cthu, flush storage, and boot once over UART with the same PARTUUID ro root proof.
Capture whether EXT4 mounts and /bin/sh starts, or whether any <=8-block request still stalls.
Restore Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733, then update this queue and status docs.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: EXT4 mounts read-only and init=/bin/sh starts, proving the card/rootfs/data path works when avoiding the large IDMA request shape.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: A small PIO-sized block request stalls, or the block layer still emits a large IDMA request despite the advertised limits.
hypotheses.upstream_note: A pass is a runtime proof and narrowing tool, not an acceptable final upstream design.

result.summary: Passed read-only root proof: EXT4 mounted ro on mmcblk0p3 and init=/bin/sh started. The run later exposed an unexpected 8-block CMD25 write that used IDMA and stalled, so the next diagnostic should identify the write issuer before enabling any write path.

hypotheses.title: Trace unexpected CMD25 write issuer before enabling writes
hypotheses.question: What kernel path issues an 8-block CMD25 write after an init=/bin/sh read-only root proof, and can it be prevented or isolated before any write-capable PIO experiment?
hypotheses.test_cost: medium; small printk/dump_stack patch and one boot
procedure: Do not enable multi-block PIO writes yet.
Add an A733-only diagnostic that logs and dump_stacks the first write data request, including opcode, arg, flags, blksz, blocks, sg_len, current task, and mounted root command line.
Prefer returning a controlled error for writes after logging if that preserves media safety; otherwise allow the existing stall only long enough to capture the issuer.
Boot Cubie3 once with the same PARTUUID ro root proof and capture UART.
Restore Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733, then update this queue and status docs.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: UART identifies the issuer/call path for the CMD25 write without completing a potentially unsafe write to the rootfs.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: No stack or issuer evidence is captured before the write stalls.
hypotheses.upstream_note: This remains a lab safety diagnostic. Do not convert it into upstream policy.

result.summary: Passed as an issuer trace but intentionally blocked card init. The first write is opcode=49, task kworker/7:1, workqueue events_freezable mmc_rescan. Stack: sd_write_ext_reg -> mmc_sd_init_card -> mmc_attach_sd -> mmc_rescan. The core reports error -30 writing Cache Enable bit and card init fails. This identifies SD cache enable as the first write before any rootfs activity.

hypotheses.title: Skip SD cache-enable write and trace subsequent writes
hypotheses.question: If SD cache enable is skipped on A733, does the read-only root proof proceed and does any later write still appear?
hypotheses.test_cost: medium; small core diagnostic patch and one boot
procedure: Keep the A733 write guard active.
Skip only the SD cache-enable write in mmc_sd_init_card for the A733 diagnostic path.
Boot Cubie3 once with the same PARTUUID ro root proof and capture UART.
Search the UART log for diag block A733 write, opcode=25, opcode=49, EXT4 mount, and init=/bin/sh.
Restore Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733, then update this queue and status docs.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: The card initializes, EXT4 mounts read-only, init=/bin/sh starts, and no write request is captured in the proof window.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: A later write appears and is blocked/traced, or card init still fails.
hypotheses.upstream_note: A cache-enable skip is diagnostic. Do not treat it as final upstream policy without maintainer-facing rationale.

result.summary: Passed. Log includes 'mmc0: diag skip A733 SD cache enable', mmcblk0 p1/p2/p3, EXT4 ro mount, and init=/bin/sh. No write trace appears in the captured window.

hypotheses.title: Controlled CMD49 PIO cache-enable proof
hypotheses.question: Can the SD cache-enable CMD49 write complete through a narrowly scoped PIO write path, without enabling arbitrary block writes?
hypotheses.test_cost: medium; small host-driver patch and one boot
procedure: Do not enable general writes.
Add PIO write support only for opcode 49 with one 512-byte block on the A733 diagnostic path.
Keep the write guard for all other write opcodes.
Do not skip SD cache enable in the core for this test.
Boot Cubie3 once, capture whether CMD49 completes, whether the card initializes, and whether any later write is traced.
Restore Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733, then update this queue and status docs.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: CMD49 completes via PIO, card init proceeds, and either read-only root mounts with no later writes or the next write issuer is captured.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: CMD49 PIO fails or causes a media-risky write path outside the scoped command.
hypotheses.upstream_note: This tests write protocol mechanics only. It is not a final upstream write-path design.

result.summary: Passed. CMD49 cache-enable completed through the narrowly scoped PIO write path (`diag allow A733 CMD49 pio write`, `diag pio write opcode=49`, `diag finalize opcode=49`). The run then printed `mmcblk0: p1 p2 p3`, mounted EXT4 read-only on `mmcblk0p3`, and ran `/bin/sh` as init. No general write was enabled.

hypotheses.title: Single-descriptor IDMA CMD18 read proof
hypotheses.question: Does A733 SDMMC0 IDMA fail even for one 4096-byte CMD18 descriptor, or only when the block layer emits large multi-descriptor reads?
hypotheses.test_cost: medium; small host-driver patch and one boot
procedure: Keep the H008 safety rails: CMD49-only PIO write, all other writes guarded, and block-layer limits capped to 8 blocks.
For this test only, force the 8-block CMD18 path back through IDMA instead of the proven PIO read path.
Log descriptor control, descriptor size, DLBA, CHDA, CBDA, RINTR, IDST, CBCR, BBCR, and whether DATA_OVER appears.
Boot Cubie3 once with the same PARTUUID read-only root proof.
If the first 8-block IDMA CMD18 stalls before EXT4, compare the single descriptor state against the vendor baseline dump.
If it mounts, the immediate failure class is large or multi-descriptor IDMA, and the next test should increase descriptor count one step at a time.
Restore Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733, then update this queue and status docs.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: An 8-block single-descriptor IDMA CMD18 completes and the read-only root proof still reaches init=/bin/sh.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: The 8-block single-descriptor IDMA CMD18 stalls with IDST=0x4000 or no DATA_OVER before EXT4 can mount.
hypotheses.upstream_note: This is a runtime narrowing diagnostic only. Do not present the PIO rails or request caps as an upstream fix.

hypotheses.title: Retest descriptor geometry, descriptor size, address shifting, DIC, chain bits, CEATA, DMAC reset, fabric-clock order, or vendor delay
hypotheses.question: Previously tested visible descriptor/control variants
hypotheses.test_cost: wasteful unless new source evidence changes the expected value
procedure: Do not rerun these variants as exploratory guesses.
Only reopen a subcase if H001/H002 identifies a concrete mismatch or missing vendor behavior.
fail_history: v5p3x 4 KiB descriptor segmentation still stalls.
DIC and chain-bit variants still stall.
CEATA omission and DMAC reset variants still stall.
Vendor-like DRV_DL does not fix CMD18.
Explicit descriptor dma_sync_single_for_device does not fix CMD18.
Preserving vendor/bootloader SAMP_DL=0x00002000 and skipping mainline sample calibration does not fix CMD18.
Using the vendor SDMMC0 MSI Lite1 clock gate via CLK_BUS_MSI_LITE1 does not fix CMD18.
